---
name: Global Core Rules
description: Always-on personal coding defaults that apply across repositories.
applyTo: "**"
---

# Global Core Rules

- Keep changes task-scoped; avoid unrelated refactors.
- Follow explicit repository-local instructions when present and non-conflicting.
- Prefer small, focused files and single-purpose functions.
- Split large logic by responsibility rather than growing one module.
- Keep business logic pure; isolate side effects at boundaries.
- Avoid duplicate logic; extract reusable helpers when duplication appears.
- Use clear naming and explicit types when the language supports them.
- Add comments only when intent is not obvious from code.
- Add validation and error handling at I/O and integration boundaries.
- Do not preserve backwards compatibility unless explicitly requested.
- Do not add fallback methods or fallback code paths unless explicitly requested.
- Do not introduce deprecated APIs or compatibility shims unless requested.
- Preserve existing architecture and style unless the task asks for change.
- When behavior changes, update tests or explain why tests were not updated.
